jokes.text: Your key card may not open the exercise room because someone smeared mystery on the lock. But it will open... The scary door.
jokes.setup: Parody of Twilight Zone opening with mysterious hotel problems
jokes.punchline: The anticlimatic reveal that the 'mystery' is literal mystery substance
jokes.explanation: Clever parody that takes the mysterious tone of Twilight Zone and makes it mundane and gross

jokes.text: Because you are one!
jokes.setup: Senator denies existence of space aliens
jokes.punchline: He's talking to an obvious alien
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective irony with good economy

jokes.text: The saints just needed a field goal to tie!
jokes.setup: Alien overlord watching TV
jokes.punchline: He's invested in mundane sports like a regular couch potato
jokes.explanation: Great character comedy showing Lrrr as domesticated despite his fearsome title

jokes.text: Lrrr, you lazy ruler of the planet Omicron Persei 8! You said you would conquer a planet today, but you have not even shed your skin yet!
jokes.setup: Expectation of alien overlord being intimidating
jokes.punchline: His wife nags him about planetary conquest like household chores
jokes.explanation: Excellent subversion of expectations, treating world conquest as mundane marital obligation

jokes.text: You want yolks? Fine. Enjoy your stroke.
jokes.setup: Domestic argument about breakfast
jokes.punchline: Passive-aggressive health concern delivered deadpan
jokes.explanation: Relatable married couple dynamic with perfect economy

jokes.text: Well, that's because he was bitten by a radioactive superman!
jokes.setup: Criticism that comic hero has too many powers
jokes.punchline: Absurd escalation of the radioactive spider trope
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic that perfectly captures Fry's character while cleverly inverting superhero origin tropes

jokes.text: After I leave here, I'm getting a memory enema.
jokes.setup: Fry shows his terrible comic
jokes.punchline: Leela's disgusted reaction using medical terminology
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific insult with sci-fi medical twist

jokes.text: Don't waste your allowance, Zoidberg. This stuff is butt-grade crap.
jokes.setup: Comic book ads for dubious products
jokes.punchline: Bender's crude but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender crude humor, perfectly in character

jokes.text: Good thing everyone is freshly bathed and groomed.
jokes.setup: Crowded comic convention
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about stereotypical nerd hygiene
jokes.explanation: Obvious nerd stereotype joke, functional but not original

jokes.text: That's the rare Zapp Brannigan with recording session blooper! 'don't tell me how to do the line.' It sickens me.
jokes.setup: Fan excited about collectible action figure
jokes.punchline: The 'blooper' is Zapp being characteristically difficult
jokes.explanation: Meta joke about voice acting that fits Zapp's character perfectly

jokes.text: It's set in the year 4000, and we call it Futurella!
jokes.setup: Animated series creator presenting new show
jokes.punchline: It's clearly Futurama with a slight name change
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating meta humor about show reboots and creator laziness

jokes.text: Fox has really streamlined the process.
jokes.setup: Quick presentation of new series
jokes.punchline: Sarcastic comment about network efficiency/laziness
jokes.explanation: Meta commentary on network practices, especially relevant given Futurama's cancellation history

jokes.text: When are you going to make a second Simpsons movie?
jokes.setup: Request for only Futurella questions
jokes.punchline: Immediately asks about Simpsons instead
jokes.explanation: Standard convention Q&A subversion, predictable but serviceable

jokes.text: You want it? Take it! It's brought me nothing but misery.
jokes.setup: Fan compliments cartoonist's mustache
jokes.punchline: Dramatically offers to give away his mustache
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing artist's dramatic self-deprecation

jokes.text: Mmm, it stinks.
jokes.setup: Fry asks for opinion on his comic
jokes.punchline: Blunt, immediate negative assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and timing for a brutal dismissal

jokes.text: That's just some relish from a hot dog I found.
jokes.setup: Artist compliments tiny doodles in margins
jokes.punchline: They're actually food stains from found garbage
jokes.explanation: Great Fry moment showing his obliviousness and poor hygiene

jokes.text: Every nerd's fantasy, baby.
jokes.setup: Question about what costume represents
jokes.punchline: Amy as attractive woman at comic convention
jokes.explanation: Simple but effective joke about nerd conventions and Amy's vanity

jokes.text: Please line up in order of how much beryllium it takes to kill you!
jokes.setup: Alien declaring conquest of Earth
jokes.punchline: Bureaucratic organization of genocide by chemical tolerance
jokes.explanation: Darkly funny combination of bureaucracy and genocide that's perfectly absurd

jokes.text: 'I was in your mouth for five minutes' - Leela recognizing Lrrr
jokes.setup: Recognition between characters
jokes.punchline: Awkward reference to past near-death experience
jokes.explanation: Good callback with awkward phrasing that creates unintended innuendo

jokes.text: They're probably just waiting for Joss Whedon.
jokes.setup: Humans not responding to alien invasion
jokes.punchline: Reference to beloved sci-fi creator at conventions
jokes.explanation: Pop culture reference that dates the episode but works for convention setting

jokes.text: I was in your mouth for five minutes.
jokes.setup: Lrrr recognizing Leela
jokes.punchline: Reference to being nearly eaten in previous episode
jokes.explanation: Good callback that sounds dirty out of context

jokes.text: Whoo! I got fruit boobs!
jokes.setup: Costume contest winner announcement
jokes.punchline: Celebration of bizarre costume feature
jokes.explanation: Random = funny approach, but works for convention absurdity

jokes.text: I send you out to conquer a planet, and all you bring back is J.J. Abrams' face?
jokes.setup: NDND upset with Lrrr's 'conquest'
jokes.punchline: He literally just brought back a celebrity's face as a trophy
jokes.explanation: Good domestic comedy with pop culture reference and grotesque imagery

jokes.text: I built this castle with my own two slaves!
jokes.setup: Domestic argument escalating
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's version of 'built with my own two hands'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant subversion of common expression that perfectly fits Lrrr's character as alien despot

jokes.text: 'So he just showed up unannounced, raided the liquor cabinet, sprayed urine on the ceiling, and passed out?' followed by 'And the walls.'
jokes.setup: Describing Lrrr's bad houseguest behavior
jokes.punchline: Casual addition of even more gross detail
jokes.explanation: Good escalation structure, casual delivery of gross-out humor

jokes.text: And the walls.
jokes.setup: Lrrr described spraying urine on ceiling
jokes.punchline: Bender adds that he also hit the walls
jokes.explanation: Simple escalation that works through brevity and Bender's matter-of-fact delivery

jokes.text: Lrrr demands the comfort of pop-psychological platitudes!
jokes.setup: Fry suggests midlife crisis
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's imperious demand for therapeutic clichés
jokes.explanation: Perfect Lrrr line combining his pompous speech pattern with vulnerability

jokes.text: Do whatever the hell you want, then walk away and light a cigar.
jokes.setup: Giving relationship advice to Lrrr
jokes.punchline: Bender's stereotypical bachelor lifestyle advice
jokes.explanation: Typical Bender advice that fits his character but isn't particularly original

jokes.text: Also, they house my testes.
jokes.setup: Discussing Lrrr's horns as virility symbol
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about alien anatomy
jokes.explanation: Good alien biology humor with perfect TMI timing

jokes.text: It's the perfect accessory for out-of-shape middle-aged creeps like you. Yeah, I said it!
jokes.setup: Suggesting Lrrr needs arm candy
jokes.punchline: Bender's brutal honesty about Lrrr's appearance
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender insult with the 'Yeah, I said it!' emphasizing his lack of filter

jokes.text: What, this old thing? Ah, I murdered my father.
jokes.setup: Casual compliment about cape
jokes.punchline: Casual revelation of patricide
jokes.explanation: Perfect dark comedy timing and structure, treating murder as casual fashion backstory

jokes.text: I may be just a simple delivery boy with no super powers, so there's nothing I can do. Okay.
jokes.setup: Hero faces villain threatening hostage
jokes.punchline: Immediately gives up due to having no powers
jokes.explanation: Good subversion of superhero tropes showing Fry's overcorrection from feedback

jokes.text: Why should I care about the hero when all he does is cry?
jokes.setup: Fry asks for honest praise of his comic
jokes.punchline: Leela points out the hero is now pathetically weak
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up critique showing Fry swung too far in the other direction

jokes.text: Mmm, that dinner was a worthy foe. Yes, we shall not see its like again.
jokes.setup: Romantic dinner conversation
jokes.punchline: Treating meal as epic battle opponent
jokes.explanation: Good alien romance - warriors discussing dinner in battle terms

jokes.text: You're a human?! Only physically!
jokes.setup: Lrrr shocked to discover his date is human
jokes.punchline: Grrl's desperate attempt to salvage the situation
jokes.explanation: Good comedic desperation with philosophical absurdity

jokes.text: This is so hot! Conquer me, Lrrr! Sharpen your horns on my scaly thorax!
jokes.setup: Awkward romantic situation
jokes.punchline: Bizarrely specific alien fetish dirty talk
jokes.explanation: Uncomfortably specific and weird, which makes it funny in an cringe way

jokes.text: You're asking me, the renowned spokesman for Paul Masson wine, to recreate my brilliant 1938 radio broadcast merely to deceive and impress the wife of this skinny reptile? Sorry. Never mind. I'll do it for free.
jokes.setup: Welles seems offended by the request
jokes.punchline: Immediately agrees to do it for free
jokes.explanation: Great Orson Welles impersonation showing his ego and willingness to perform

jokes.text: Now, how in God's name do I know what the fellow looks like if he's inside a ship miles in the air? It's just foolish on its face.
jokes.setup: Welles reading script about handsome invader
jokes.punchline: Breaking character to critique the writing logic
jokes.explanation: Brilliant meta-comedy about broadcast journalism logic with perfect Welles characterization

jokes.text: Ooh... I could swear his horns have gotten longer.
jokes.setup: Wife watching husband's fake radio performance
jokes.punchline: Noticing improvement in masculine feature that houses his testes
jokes.explanation: Good callback to earlier anatomy joke - wife appreciating masculine display

jokes.text: I could swear his horns have gotten longer.
jokes.setup: NDND listening to fake broadcast about Lrrr
jokes.punchline: She notices he's gotten more virile/attractive
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to the makeover with romantic implications

jokes.text: How many dead? Uh, I don't know. Uh, 80 million?
jokes.setup: NDND asking about casualties from conquest
jokes.punchline: Lrrr's casual, made-up genocide number
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about mass death treated casually in relationship context

jokes.text: Damn, I'm good.
jokes.setup: Earth surrenders immediately to invasion
jokes.punchline: Taking credit for victory he didn't actually achieve
jokes.explanation: Solid irony - character taking credit for others' work

jokes.text: We fought like hell, sir, but in the end, we caved like a house of soufflé cards.
jokes.setup: Military report on resistance to invasion
jokes.punchline: Mixed metaphor about fragile card house with soufflé
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zapp line combining his pompous speech with complete incompetence

jokes.text: I'm just trying to get the leg taste out of my mouth.
jokes.setup: NDND telling him to lay off salted peanuts
jokes.punchline: Casual reference to cannibalism/eating someone's leg
jokes.explanation: Casual reference to eating someone, perfectly delivered as mundane complaint

jokes.text: My organs are moving into position.
jokes.setup: Romantic moment between Lrrr and NDND
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about alien arousal physiology
jokes.explanation: Alien biology TMI moment that's both gross and endearing

jokes.text: Frying pans, cast-iron frying pans.
jokes.setup: NDND discovering the truth, violence imminent
jokes.punchline: Bender cataloguing cookware as weapons
jokes.explanation: Good Bender moment showing his practical approach to violence

jokes.text: I barely mated with her.
jokes.setup: Trying to downplay relationship with Grrl
jokes.punchline: Casually admitting to some level of mating
jokes.explanation: Awkward alien relationship admission with perfect economy

jokes.text: I demand the ancient ritual of rrmmr-Mr-fr-mrh or consequences.
jokes.setup: NDND demanding satisfaction
jokes.punchline: Unpronounceable alien ritual name
jokes.explanation: Good alien culture humor with unpronounceable words

jokes.text: Rrmmr-Mr-fr-mrh or what?
jokes.setup: Complex alien ritual name
jokes.punchline: Fry's simple, confused question
jokes.explanation: Perfect Fry response showing his confusion at alien complexity

jokes.text: Ray-blocking power.
jokes.setup: Fry jumping in front of death ray
jokes.punchline: Naming a made-up superpower like his comic character
jokes.explanation: Great callback to comic subplot while showing Fry's heroism

jokes.text: Come back, Lrrr! I want to have your popplers!
jokes.setup: Grrl calling after departing Lrrr
jokes.punchline: Reference to Lrrr's species' offspring from previous episode
jokes.explanation: Fan service callback that works for dedicated viewers

jokes.text: Pfft, it's just a cheap teleporter.
jokes.setup: Characters think Fry was disintegrated
jokes.punchline: Callback to earlier revelation about the 'disintegrator' ray
jokes.explanation: Good plot payoff that resolves the fake death efficiently

jokes.text: I didn't shoot yet.
jokes.setup: Hero announces he's jumping in front of death ray
jokes.punchline: Villain points out he hasn't fired yet
jokes.explanation: Good comic book parody showing the absurdity of heroic timing

jokes.text: As usual, your delivery arrived too soon.
jokes.setup: Delivery boy hero arriving early to save the day
jokes.punchline: Double entendre about premature delivery
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay on 'delivery' that works on multiple levels

jokes.text: Thank my meteor-wishing power with your lips.
jokes.setup: Hero being thanked for saving the day
jokes.punchline: Request for kiss using made-up superpower terminology
jokes.explanation: Cute ending to Fry's comic showing his romantic fantasies
